deployment_question: How does the model extrapolate to molecules or materials in extreme property regimes?
definition: Hold out samples in high-value, low-value, or otherwise rare property regimes to test extrapolation beyond the central training distribution.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: targets low support along the target/property axis rather than only structural novelty

implementation_recipe: Define the property threshold or quantile before model fitting.
Train on central or non-extreme regimes and test on the held-out extreme regime.
Report whether the property itself was used to define the split and whether this is allowed for the study objective.
quality_gates: Distinguish target extrapolation from covariate novelty.
Report target support and calibration in extreme bins.
Avoid using test labels for model selection.
failure_modes: Splitting on the target can change the task into extrapolation rather than deployment simulation.
Extreme bins can have too few samples.
Regression metrics can be dominated by target range.

citations.title: BOOM: Benchmarking Out-Of-distribution Molecular Property Predictions
citations.evidence: Introduces OOD molecular property prediction tasks where models struggle under broader distribution shifts.

citations.title: Known Unknowns: Out-of-Distribution Property Prediction in Materials and Molecules
citations.evidence: Studies property prediction outside the training distribution, including extreme-value regimes.
